Explore CountriesRead Tax Guides

Featured Countries

United States

North America

The United States has the world's largest economy and the most complex tax system. The federal government imposes a progressive income tax, payroll taxes, corporate taxes, capital gains taxes, and estate taxes. State and local governments add their own tax layers. Tax residents are taxed on worldwide income, while non-residents are taxed only on US-source income. The tax year follows the calendar year, with returns due April 15th.

Explore taxes →

India

Asia

India has one of the world's largest and most complex tax systems. The Income Tax Act of 1961 governs direct taxes, while the Goods and Services Tax (GST) Act unifies indirect taxation. India uses a self-assessment system where taxpayers calculate and declare their own tax liability. The tax year runs from April 1 to March 31, with returns due July 31 for individuals. India offers two tax regimes: the old regime with deductions and exemptions, and the new regime with lower slab rates but limited deductions.

Explore taxes →

Hong Kong

Asia

Hong Kong operates a territorial tax system with low tax rates, making it one of the most attractive jurisdictions for international business and investment. Only profits arising from or derived from Hong Kong are subject to tax. The city has no capital gains tax, no VAT, and no sales tax. The two main direct taxes are Salaries Tax (on employment income) and Profits Tax (on business profits).

Explore taxes →

Singapore

Asia

Singapore has a competitive, low-tax regime designed to attract businesses and investments. The country operates a territorial tax system where only income sourced in Singapore is taxed. Resident individuals pay progressive income tax rates from 0% to 24%, while non-residents are taxed at flat rates or 24% whichever is lower. Singapore has no capital gains tax and no inheritance tax, making it one of the most tax-friendly jurisdictions globally.

Explore taxes →

Indonesia

Asia

Indonesia has a self-assessment tax system where taxpayers are responsible for calculating and reporting their own tax obligations. The country imposes progressive personal income tax rates from 5% to 35%, and corporate tax at 22%. Indonesia also levies Value Added Tax (VAT) at 11%. The tax year follows the calendar year (January 1 to December 31), with individual tax returns due by April 30th.

Explore taxes →

Malaysia

Asia

Malaysia has a comprehensive tax system with progressive individual income tax rates from 0% to 30%, a flat corporate tax rate of 24%, and Sales and Service Tax (SST). The tax year in Malaysia runs from January 1 to December 31, with individual tax returns due by April 30th. Malaysia offers various tax reliefs and deductions including those for education, medical expenses, and home ownership.

Explore taxes →

How TaxPortal Helps You

Plan Your Taxes Confidently

Understand your tax obligations before they become surprises. Our calculators provide instant, accurate estimates using the latest tax brackets, rates, and rules for each country. Whether you are a salaried employee, freelancer, business owner, or investor, we have tools designed for your situation.

Compare Tax Regimes

Not sure which tax regime is best for you? Our comparison tools help you evaluate the old vs new tax regimes side by side, so you can make informed decisions about your tax strategy and maximize your take-home pay.

Understand Your Deductions

From Section 80C investments in India to the standard deduction in the US, our guides and calculators help you identify every deduction and credit you may be eligible for. Don't leave money on the table.

Stay compliant, stay informed

Tax laws change every year. We keep our calculators and guides updated with the latest rates, thresholds, and regulations so you can stay compliant and avoid penalties.

About TaxPortal

TaxPortal is a free, comprehensive online platform dedicated to making international tax information accessible and actionable. We provide accurate tax calculators, detailed guides, and up-to-date tax rate information for individuals and businesses across multiple countries.

Every calculator on our platform is built with precision, using official tax brackets, withholding tables, and statutory rates sourced from government authorities. Our content is reviewed by tax professionals to ensure accuracy and relevance.

We believe that tax planning should not be a source of anxiety. By providing clear, well-structured tools and educational content, we empower users to take control of their financial future and make informed tax decisions.

Disclaimer: TaxPortal is for informational and educational purposes only. It does not constitute tax, legal, or financial advice. Always consult a qualified tax professional for advice specific to your situation.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a tax calculator?

A tax calculator is a tool that estimates your tax liability based on your income, deductions, filing status, and applicable tax rates. TaxPortal provides free, browser-based calculators for countries including the United States, India, the United Kingdom, Singapore, and more.

How accurate are TaxPortal calculators?

Our calculators use official tax brackets, rates, and rules from each country's tax authority. They provide accurate estimates based on the information you input. However, they are for informational purposes only and should not replace professional tax advice.

Do I need to create an account to use the calculators?

No. All TaxPortal calculators are completely free to use and do not require an account or personal information. All calculations are performed locally in your browser.

How often are the tax rates updated?

We update our calculators and guides as soon as new tax rates, brackets, or rules are announced by the relevant tax authorities. We strive to reflect the latest changes for every tax year.

Can I compare old and new tax regimes?

Yes. For countries that offer multiple tax regimes, such as India, our calculators allow you to compare both regimes side by side to determine which one minimizes your tax liability.

Is TaxPortal free to use?

Yes, all basic calculators and educational content on TaxPortal are completely free. There are no hidden fees or subscription requirements.
